headline: Micron Earnings Were Extraordinary. The Stock Is Dropping.
summary: Micron Technology  reported exceptional second-quarter earnings results Wednesday afternoon.  Adjusted earnings-per-share soared to $12.20, well beyond Wall Street’s consensus estimate of $9.19, and up from $1.56 last year.  Guidance for the third quarter is equally stunning, with a projection of $33.5 billion in sales and a whopping 81% gross margin, even better than  Nvidia’s  Adjusted EPS are expected to rise to $19.15, a 903% jump from the year before.

headline: Micron Q2 earnings, Q3 outlook surpass Wall Street's estimates
summary: Micron (MU) reported second quarter results on Wednesday after the closing bell. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $12.20 (compared to analyst estimates of $9.00), and adjusted revenue came in at $23.86 billion (compared to analyst estimates of $19.74 billion). Micron's third quarter earnings and revenue forecasts blew Wall Street's estimates out of the water. Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton takes a closer look at the numbers.

headline: Micron (NASDAQ:MU) Delivers Strong Q1 Numbers
summary: Memory chips maker Micron (NYSE:MU) reported Q1 CY2026 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 196% year on year to $23.86 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($33.5 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 41.4% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $12.20 per share was 40.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($33.5 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 41.4% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $12.20 per share was 40.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Memory chipmaker Micron's revenue beats estimates on demand surge from AI boom

The resulting growth in AI data center capacity is fueling a sharp rise in demand for advanced ‌memory and storage.

headline: Micron Earnings Face High Expectations as Its Stock Keeps Rising
summary: Micron shares have soared more than 60% to start the year, the sixth-best performance in the S&P 500 Index, after jumping 239% in 2025.  The biggest US maker of memory chips is benefiting from heavy spending on artificial intelligence computing that’s driving demand for memory and storage components, as are high-flying peers Sandisk Corp. and Western Digital Corp.

headline: Samsung Weighs Multi-Year Deals to Ease Memory Chip Crunch Fears
summary: SK Hynix Inc., Samsung and Micron Technology Inc. together dominate the supply of memory chips globally.  The three have shifted production in recent years toward a specialized form of memory intended for use in Nvidia Corp.’s in-demand AI accelerators, leading to a shortfall in output of more conventional storage.

headline: Micron’s New HBM4 And PCIe Gen6 SSDs Test Lofty AI Valuation
summary: Micron Technology has started volume production of its latest HBM4 memory aimed at Nvidia's next generation AI platforms. The company is also rolling out what it describes as the industry's first mass produced PCIe Gen6 data center SSDs for AI workloads. These launches follow the completion of Micron's acquisition and expansion of a major Taiwan facility focused on advanced DRAM and HBM for AI applications. For investors watching NasdaqGS:MU, the news comes during a period of strong recent...
